items.text: Following the COVID-19 pandemic, governments have demonstrated a greater interest in eviction moratoria and strict housing controls.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new regulatory risk regarding eviction moratoria and housing controls following COVID-19, which is a concrete and material risk not previously mentioned.

items.text: We may be legally required to, or otherwise agree to, restructure tenants’ rent obligations on less favorable terms than those currently in place.
items.rationale: Identifies a specific new risk that the company may be forced to restructure tenant rent obligations unfavorably, a concrete business impact.

items.text: This could lead to lower profitability and market fluctuations that may affect our ability to obtain necessary funds for our business.
items.rationale: This is a vague, generic statement about lower profitability and market fluctuations without specific new risk detail, functioning as boilerplate consequence language.

items.text: Some employment claims such as California private attorney general actions (“PAGA”) or class actions are also not insurable.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific legal risk category (PAGA and class actions) that is explicitly uninsurable, a concrete new exposure.

items.text: Additionally, remote and hybrid working arrangements at our company (and at many third-party providers) also increase cybersecurity risks due to the challenges associated with managing remote computing assets and security vulnerabilities that are present in many non-corporate and home networks.
items.rationale: Builds on an existing cybersecurity risk by adding specific detail about remote/hybrid work increasing vulnerabilities, expanding rather than introducing a wholly new risk.

items.text: Furthermore, we use artificial intelligence technologies in our business and there are significant information technology risks involved in maintaining and deploying these technologies.
items.rationale: Introduces a new specific risk related to the use of artificial intelligence technologies and associated IT risks.

items.text: There can be no assurance that our investments in such technologies will always enhance our services or be beneficial to our business, including our efficiency or profitability.
items.rationale: Generic cautionary statement that technology investments may not always be beneficial, lacking specific new risk content.

items.text: Anti-ESG advocates, including certain state attorneys general outside of the Company’s current geographic locations, have also brought legal challenges regarding corporate climate initiatives and commitments.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new legal/political risk from anti-ESG advocates and attorneys general challenging climate commitments.
